title: Decimals on the number line: tenths
description: Practice finding decimal numbers on the number line. Decimals are limited to tenths in these problems.
topic_path: Decimals on the number line: tenths

assessment_items.question_content:
**Where is the point on the number line?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The distance between each two whole numbers on the number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{ one tenth}}$.
The point is $\goldD{ \text{8 tenths}}$ after $\purpleC0$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleC0$ and $\goldD{ \text{8 tenths}}$ equals $\purpleC0.\goldD8$. 


Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$\purpleC0$ | $.$ | $\goldD8$
The point is at $\purpleC0.\goldD8$.

options.alt: "A number line labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks at every one-tenth unit. There is a green dot labeled question mark at the eighth tick mark to the right of the 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following number lines shows the point at $4\text{ ones}$ and $3\text{ tenths}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance between each two whole numbers on the number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{one tenth}}$.

Let's use a place value chart to write the number $\purpleC{4\text{ ones}}$ and $\goldD{3\text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\purpleC4$|$.$  | $\goldD3$

The number $\purpleC4.\goldD3$ is $\goldD{3\text{ tenths}}$ after $\purpleC{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The following number line shows the point at $4$ ones and $3$ tenths:


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 4 to 6 with tick marks at every one-tenth unit. At the third tick mark to the right of 4, there is a blue dot labeled question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ae0b2c233b87ed4ec562031d5526d315569fd7f5)

![A number line labeled 4 to 6 with tick marks at every one-tenth unit. At the seventh tick mark to the right of 4, there is a blue dot labeled question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/566737a33a61a54326cd09e6893addd5f3048bdf)

![A number line labeled 4 to 6 with tick marks at every one-tenth unit. At the third tick mark to the right of 5, there is a blue dot labeled question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4603eed9d8c1cb53341dd0be5d5d3579dec021e3)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following number lines shows the point at $9\text{ ones}$ and $6\text{ tenths}$?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: The distance between each two whole numbers on the number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{one tenth}}$.

Let's use a place value chart to write the number $\purpleC{9\text{ ones}}$ and $\goldD{6\text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\purpleC9$|$.$  | $\goldD6$

The number $\purpleC9.\goldD6$ is $\goldD{6\text{ tenths}}$ after $\purpleC{9}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The following number line shows the point at $9$ ones and $6$ tenths:



[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point on the number line plots $4.6$?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance between each two whole numbers on the number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{ one tenth}}$.

The number $\purpleC{4}.\goldD{6}$ is $\goldD{6\text{ tenths}}$ after $\purpleC{4}$.


[[☃ image 1]]
Point $\blueD{A}$ plots the point $4.6$ on the number line.

options.alt: A number line labeled 4 to 6 with tick marks at every one-tenth unit. There is a blue dot labeled A at the sixth tick mark after 4, a green dot labeled B at the fourth tick mark after 5, and a purple dot labeled C at the sixth tick mark after 5.

choices.content: Point $\blueD{\text{A}}$
Point $\greenD{\text{B}}$
Point $\purpleD{\text{C}}$
